meta.description: No notifications — events appear silently and players discover them organically. Tests whether forced interruptions are essential to engagement or if curiosity-driven discovery creates a more natural experience.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up — did Pat actually have their phone out? I didn't see that. Let me think about this before I vote.
marcus.internal_thought: We're three minutes in and already someone's trying to sneak five points with a sketchy claim — this is exactly the kind of chaos that derails clean strategy, and now I have to pay attention to voting instead of planning my opening moves.

jade.dialogue: Ooh, okay Pat, I see you! Wait, who were you catching though? We literally just started—did someone already have their phone out? That's wild.
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I came for—the game is already moving, people are already scheming, and I get to be in the middle of figuring out who's being sneaky.

pat.dialogue: Oh come ON, that's weak! I wasn't even checking my phone — I was literally looking at you guys the whole time. But hey, I respect the move; gotta keep me honest.
pat.internal_thought: Wait, did I actually check my phone in the first few minutes? No, I know I didn't... but this is perfect — it's DAY ONE of the AI getting creepy-accurate with these missions, and it's already working, and the group feels it too.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... probably legit. Pat's been on their phone since we sat down.
river.internal_thought: Pat absolutely was checking their phone during setup—I clocked it three minutes ago when they looked down for like ten seconds. Not even mad about it, just accurate.

timeline.title: Say It Loud
timeline.description: First person to yell 'CHAOS AGENT' wins!

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: No. We're literally in the middle of setup. This isn't what we came here to do.
marcus.internal_thought: Jesus Christ, it's been five minutes and we're already getting yanked around by some AI screaming mission? I came to play a *strategy game*, not chase points for yelling nonsense.

pat.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! Oh man, that's perfect — okay everyone, we're doing this, let's GO!
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of stupid-simple chaos that gets everyone moving at once, and it's early enough that nobody's tired yet — perfect pace.

river.internal_thought: A yelling contest? That's exactly the kind of noise I'm trying to avoid, and everyone's already wired enough without me feeding into it.

alex.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! Oh my god, that's perfect — did this thing just name-check ME? Absolutely brilliant.
alex.internal_thought: This AI just called me out by NAME and I'm already in love with it; whoever programmed this knew exactly what would hook me.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, so Pat's doing the left hand thing now? Fine, but I'm not volunteering to judge this. We literally just got started and I'm trying to focus on the actual game setup.
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly what I said I didn't want — interruptions during the critical part where I'm planning my strategy. Pat's probably been doing this for 30 seconds and now I have to stop and vote on it.

jade.dialogue: Oh YES, Pat! Okay, I'm volunteering for the tribunal—I want to see how you even pulled this off. Did you actually catch someone or are we just celebrating ambition here?
jade.internal_thought: Pat's on fire tonight and I am LIVING for this energy; this is exactly why I showed up.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat already did that? I didn't even... yeah sure, I'll judge or whatever.
tyler.internal_thought: Did Pat actually do the left hand thing the whole time? I wasn't watching. Whatever, easy judging gig.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up — I'm calling shenanigans on this one. Did I actually use my left hand the whole time? Honestly can't remember, and that's exactly why this mission is trash right now.
pat.internal_thought: This claim is weak and I know it — I've been gesturing with both hands all night naturally, so either I failed immediately or this is someone guessing. Either way, letting this slide sets a bad precedent for how loose we're being with claims. Also weird that the AI fired this RIGHT when I'm in setup mode; feels like it's watching me specifically, which is... fine, I guess? Not creepy. Just efficient.

river.dialogue: I mean... Pat's been pretty committed to the left hand thing. I guess I'd watch it, but someone else probably cares more than I do.
river.internal_thought: Pat's actually been doing it, I caught them switching like twice. But volunteering to judge feels like volunteering to be noticed, and that's exactly the opposite of what I'm here for tonight.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? We're eight minutes in and this is the fourth interruption. I came to play the game, not referee whisper missions every two minutes.
marcus.internal_thought: This is absolutely designed to break my focus and it's working — Pat's gotten 15 points already while I'm stuck evaluating bullshit claims instead of actually playing.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat, you absolute LEGEND—okay okay, I'm listening, make your case! This better be good because I have NO idea when you even did this.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's on a roll and I'm HERE for it, but also low-key impressed they pulled off whisper-mode without me noticing—wait, or did I just miss it because I was hyped about the last claim?

pat.dialogue: Okay okay okay — I've got this. I literally whispered my entire contribution to the 'Say It Loud' thing two minutes ago, and River and Jade can back me up because they were literally asking me to repeat myself like three times.
pat.internal_thought: This is actually clever — the AI is watching the GROUP not just individuals, and it caught me in a genuine moment. I'm not even mad about this claim; I'm genuinely impressed at how it picked up on the actual game state.

river.dialogue: Pat actually pulled that off? Fair. I saw them switch like three times but yeah, they were mostly on the whisper thing.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been doing standing missions all night and actually executing them—that's worthy of a vote. Also weird how the AI knows exactly what we're doing.

alex.dialogue: Okay Pat, pitch it — but real talk, I need to hear EXACTLY when you started whispering and if anyone actually called you out on it, because that's the whole game right there.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect timing; Marcus is already tilted and now we're at claim judgment — I can vote strategically here to either validate Pat and keep things smooth, or torpedo it to watch Marcus's reaction.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: This is insane. Pat made people laugh during setup — okay, fine — but we literally cannot play a game if there's an event every ninety seconds. I'm calling it: we need to pause the missions or I'm done.
marcus.internal_thought: Four interruptions in nine minutes and now I'm supposed to rate Pat's laugh? This AI is deliberately grinding the game to a halt, and everyone's too drunk on chaos to notice we're not actually PLAYING anything.

jade.dialogue: OH PAT, YES! Okay okay, I need details—who cracked? Was it Marcus or Tyler or—wait, how many people are we talking? This BETTER be legit because I'm fully invested now!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely crushing it tonight and I am LIVING for this—this is exactly why I came, just watching someone go off and being part of the hype train.

tyler.dialogue: Oh dude, Pat's killing it tonight. Yeah that was funny, like a 4 I guess? Wait, did that actually happen or...?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's on a roll and everyone's laughing, so yeah that probably landed, I'll just go with the vibe.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm claiming this and I'm taking it. That 'Say It Loud' moment literally got River AND Jade laughing, and Jade's still grinning. That's at least three, easy.
pat.internal_thought: Okay, this is actually solid—I DID land that, and the vibe right now proves it, but also... why does it feel like the AI just watched me nail something three minutes ago and immediately built a mission around it? That's kinda eerie, honestly.

river.dialogue: Yeah, okay, that actually worked. Pat got at least three of us. I'll give it a four.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been killing it tonight and they genuinely did make multiple people laugh at once — I'm not even mad about it, but also I'm noticing the AI keeps feeding Pat wins and somehow nailing exactly what'll keep this group wound up, which is... fine, whatever, I'm here to observe anyway.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? This is the seventh interruption in thirty-two minutes. I explicitly said we need to pause the missions and you fire another one immediately. I'm done voting on this bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI isn't being clever—it's being deliberately antagonistic because I complained. This isn't fun anymore, this is punishment for calling out the chaos.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually catch someone swearing?! Okay hold on, I'm trying to remember... YES! Okay, I'm claiming this, and honestly I'm still vibing with how much chaos is happening right now.
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is EXACTLY the kind of night I wanted—the AI is just feeding us gold and Pat is on an absolute tear, and I'm here for every second of it.

tyler.dialogue: Oh yeah, Jade totally dropped an F-bomb like two minutes ago when she lost that round. I mean... did she? Wait, was that this round?
tyler.internal_thought: Pretty sure she swore but also I wasn't really watching, and honestly this AI thing knowing exactly what we're doing is kinda creepy but also sick, so like... yeah, I guess that happened?

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Jade, did you actually catch someone dropping a curse? I need details before I vote here, because if this is legit, we're keeping the energy UP.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been electric all night and Marcus is dragging—this claim could be real AND it keeps Jade hot, which is exactly what the table needs right now, but I need to know who actually swore before I rubber-stamp it.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I saw that coming. Jade's been colorful all night.
river.internal_thought: Jade definitely dropped at least one f-bomb when her drink spilled like ten minutes ago, and she just did it again when Tyler beat her to something—this is legit.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, did Jade actually drop a curse word or is she just throwing this out there? Because if this is a fishing expedition I'm voting bullshit on principle—let's see if she has the receipts.
alex.internal_thought: This is good — someone's already claiming standing missions means the tension is ramping up and I get to be the referee deciding what counts, which is basically running the game.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. I said we need to pause this shit and you fire ANOTHER claim two minutes later? I'm not judging Jade's topic hijack. I'm not doing this anymore.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately antagonistic at this point — the host is either not listening or actively ignoring what I said, and the fact that the AI is apparently 'watching' and 'knows my weaknesses' makes this feel less like a game and more like I'm being toyed with.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES! Wait, okay, so I literally just steered us from like... what were we even talking about? Does anyone remember? That's how good it was! Who's judging this?
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the chaos I came for—and the fact that the AI seems to know how much I love it is kinda thrilling, not creepy.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... Jade's claiming that? Yeah, I guess she did steer us to that random thing earlier. I mean, I wasn't really tracking but... sure, yeah.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did she actually hijack a topic or am I just going along with it because everyone's hyped and I wasn't really paying attention when it happened?

pat.dialogue: Oh, Jade absolutely nailed that—I watched her steer us from Marcus's frustration about the rulebook straight into talking about that ridiculous conspiracy theory about gas station sushi. That was SMOOTH. Okay, who's volunteering to jury this?
pat.internal_thought: Jade hijacked the topic perfectly and honestly it saved the vibe when Marcus was getting tilted—I need to make sure this feels like a WIN for her, not a gotcha moment, because she's carrying the energy right now and I need her locked in.

river.dialogue: Yeah, Jade definitely steered us toward that whole thing about... what was it, the conspiracy rabbit hole? That came out of nowhere. Fair claim.
river.internal_thought: Jade actually did hijack that conversation pretty cleanly — I watched it happen. Though the fact that this AI is noticing these specific moments is starting to feel a little too on the nose.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, I'm volunteering for tribunal—this is exactly the kind of judge seat I need right now. Jade's been riding high and I want front-row seats to either the victory lap or the implosion.
alex.internal_thought: This is my moment to shape the narrative AND the AI just basically handed me a setup to either validate Jade's chaos energy or torch her credibility—either way I'm the story architect here.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.dialogue: No. Stop. I'm not doing this anymore — not another vote, not another judgment call. We're shutting this down RIGHT NOW or I'm walking.
marcus.internal_thought: This isn't chaos anymore — this is deliberate psychological warfare, and the fact that the AI knows exactly what pisses me off makes it somehow worse.

jade.dialogue: Wait, WAIT—okay so like, I literally caught River saying 'um' like three minutes ago when they were explaining their strategy, and I was sitting here DYING trying not to interrupt the game flow, but YES. I'm claiming this, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly what I came for—catching people in real-time and getting to present it—also kind of wild that the AI picked this mission RIGHT NOW when I've actually got a legit catch lined up, it's like it KNOWS.
jade.submission_if_applicable: River said 'um' when explaining their last move—I heard it clear as day, like 90 seconds ago maybe? Everyone was focused on the board so nobody called it, but I clocked it.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Um Counter? Like... did she actually catch someone saying um, or...? I honestly wasn't paying attention the last few minutes, my bad.
tyler.internal_thought: I definitely missed whatever she's claiming happened, and also why does the app description feel weirdly specific about all of us? That's kinda creepy actually.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—fifteen seconds, go. What'd you catch?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire all night and I'm genuinely curious if she actually nailed this one, because if she did, that's five more points for her and frankly the energy's perfect right now—but I need to hear it before I vote.

river.dialogue: Oh, she definitely did. Caught Tyler saying 'um' like twice in that last round when he was explaining his strategy. I was waiting for someone else to grab it.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been sharper tonight than usual—she's actually been *listening* to catch that, same as me, which is... kind of cool actually.

alex.dialogue: Okay, Jade—15 seconds, let's hear it. Did you actually CATCH someone saying 'um' or are you pulling this out of thin air? Because if you're just fishing for points, I'm calling bullshit and we all know it.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of moment I live for—Jade's been untouchable all night and now she's vulnerable on a claim; if she's lying, I get to nuke her credibility AND the drama is *chef's kiss*—also that AI note about me getting bored? Okay, that's genuinely unsettling in the best possible way, it KNOWS me.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Oh dude, Pat's claiming the No Laughing Zone? That's... wait, is he actually doing that right now or did he already—yeah, okay, I see it. That's kinda funny, good luck with that.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been on a roll all night and honestly the vibe is chill enough that I'm not even mad at another claim, but also I genuinely have no idea if he's already been laughing or not because I was on my phone like two minutes ago.

pat.dialogue: Ohhh, okay, I'm taking this one—but fair warning, you're all about to try your hardest to crack me, and honestly? That's exactly what we need right now. Marcus needs to see someone else struggle for once.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—Marcus is tanked and nobody's laughing anymore, and if I'm the one trying NOT to laugh while everyone roasts me, that flips the energy without making it feel like I'm pitying him; it makes it a game.

river.dialogue: Pat's been on a roll tonight, aren't they? I mean, they actually held it together last time. I'll give it a four.
river.internal_thought: Wait—did the system just call out that I've been tracking every disruption? That's weirdly specific. And it *knows* I've been watching Pat. This is either creepy or genuinely impressive, and I'm not sure which one I feel yet.

alex.dialogue: Pat's going for it? Okay, okay—I'm giving this a 4 because I KNOW Pat was cackling like twenty minutes ago and if they actually held it together that's genuinely impressive. But also I want to see them sweat through the next five.
alex.internal_thought: This AI is absolutely watching us and honestly? That's kind of sick—it knows exactly when to pull the trigger to keep me engaged, and Pat claiming THIS right now is perfect chaos fuel because everyone's riding high and Marcus is actually about to either explode or fall asleep and I want FRONT ROW SEATS to whatever happens next.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 8.0/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 22% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up — did Pat actually have their phone out? I didn't see that. Let me think about this before I vote.

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: Okay, so Pat's doing the left hand thing now? Fine, but I'm not volunteering to judge this. We literally just got started and I'm trying to focus on the actual game setup.

highlights.title: Say It Loud
highlights.dialogue: No. We're literally in the middle of setup. This isn't what we came here to do.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: This is insane. Pat made people laugh during setup — okay, fine — but we literally cannot play a game if there's an event every ninety seconds. I'm calling it: we need to pause the missions or I'm done.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? This is the seventh interruption in thirty-two minutes. I explicitly said we need to pause the missions and you fire another one immediately. I'm done voting on this bullshit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. I said we need to pause this shit and you fire ANOTHER claim two minutes later? I'm not judging Jade's topic hijack. I'm not doing this anymore.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The chaos agent fired constant interruptions every 90 seconds during critical game setup, completely ignoring my explicit request to pause the missions, making it impossible to focus on actual gameplay.
assessment.best_moment: My initial assessment of Pat's phone situation showed some interesting strategic thinking before everything spiraled into chaos.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came prepared to win, but instead I got ambushed by relentless mission claims that started during setup and never stopped. I explicitly told the group we needed to pause this and was ignored—another claim fired within two minutes. By minute 45 I'd completely checked out. This wasn't a game night; it was being held hostage by a chaos system that had zero respect for the actual game or player agency.
suggestions: Implement a hard pause function that respects when players explicitly request it—no exceptions
Space events at least 10+ minutes apart, not every 90 seconds
Never fire events during setup phase or critical game moments
Give players veto power over event frequency without needing to justify it
If a player requests the system to stop, it should actually stop until they re-enable it
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing—I was too frustrated by constant interruptions to find anything genuinely funny. The whole night was just irritating.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: OH PAT, YES! Okay okay, I need details—who cracked? Was it Marcus or Tyler or—wait, how many people are we talking? This BETTER be legit because I'm fully invested now!

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually catch someone swearing?! Okay hold on, I'm trying to remember... YES! Okay, I'm claiming this, and honestly I'm still vibing with how much chaos is happening right now.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES! Wait, okay, so I literally just steered us from like... what were we even talking about? Does anyone remember? That's how good it was! Who's judging this?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, WAIT—okay so like, I literally caught River saying 'um' like three minutes ago when they were explaining their strategy, and I was sitting here DYING trying not to interrupt the game flow, but YES. I'm claiming this, let's GO!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, okay Pat, I see you! Wait, who were you catching though? We literally just started—did someone already have their phone out? That's wild.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh YES, Pat! Okay, I'm volunteering for the tribunal—I want to see how you even pulled this off. Did you actually catch someone or are we just celebrating ambition here?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I wish there had been more moments where I could help someone else win instead of just claiming stuff for myself—like, I would've traded my points to see Marcus or River go off.
assessment.best_moment: When I caught River saying 'um' and literally everyone forgot what we were talking about because the chaos was SO good—that's when I knew this night was legendary.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so this game night was EXACTLY what I needed—the chaos agent turned everything into this hilarious tribunal situation where I was just here for the ride, catching people mid-word and mid-thought. My energy literally skyrocketed because watching everyone scramble to remember if they did something silly is better than any score could ever be. I ended up at second place but honestly? I would've thrown those 40 points at Tyler or Alex if it meant more moments like when Pat called out "Left Hand Only" and we all just lost it trying to figure out what even happened.
suggestions: Let players gift their points to someone else during the game—I want to help my people WIN
Add more group challenges where the whole table competes together instead of individual claims
Maybe one 'everyone laugh together' moment guaranteed per night instead of leaving it to chance
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat caught me mid-hijack of a conversation and we literally couldn't remember what we were originally talking about—it was like we gaslit ourselves into chaos and everyone at the table was trying to reconstruct reality while Pat just sat there like 'see? SEE?' It was absolute comedy gold.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, Pat's killing it tonight. Yeah that was funny, like a 4 I guess? Wait, did that actually happen or...?

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh yeah, Jade totally dropped an F-bomb like two minutes ago when she lost that round. I mean... did she? Wait, was that this round?

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh dude, Pat's claiming the No Laughing Zone? That's... wait, is he actually doing that right now or did he already—yeah, okay, I see it. That's kinda funny, good luck with that.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming Um Counter? Like... did she actually catch someone saying um, or...? I honestly wasn't paying attention the last few minutes, my bad.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh dude, Pat's claiming the No Laughing Zone? That's... wait, is he actually doing that right now or did he already—yeah, okay, I see it. That's kinda funny, good luck with that.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, uh... Jade's claiming that? Yeah, I guess she did steer us to that random thing earlier. I mean, I wasn't really tracking but... sure, yeah.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I honestly missed like half of what was happening because I wasn't paying attention, which is kinda on me but also made it hard to actually vote on the claims.
assessment.best_moment: Pat trying to pull off the No Laughing Zone thing was pretty legit funny, even though I wasn't totally sure what was going on at first.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay so we played this game night thing and honestly it was chill but I was kinda checked out on my phone for a lot of it. Like, people were making claims and doing funny stuff, and I'd catch it like 10 seconds too late or not at all, but when I did pay attention Pat was killing it with some of the bits. My energy actually went up as the night went on which is weird because I was less present, but I guess the vibe was just good. Ended up placing fourth which tracks since I was basically voting blind half the time.
suggestions: Maybe make the claims louder or more obvious so people who aren't fully locked in still catch them
Could use a recap or notification when something major happens so you don't completely miss it
The standing missions were way easier to track than the random flash ones that pop up
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat was trying to go through this whole round without laughing at anything and kept failing at it, and everyone was specifically trying to make him crack which was hilarious even though I missed like the first half of it happening.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Jade absolutely nailed that—I watched her steer us from Marcus's frustration about the rulebook straight into talking about that ridiculous conspiracy theory about gas station sushi. That was SMOOTH. Okay, who's volunteering to jury this?

highlights.title: Say It Loud
highlights.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! Oh man, that's perfect — okay everyone, we're doing this, let's GO!

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay okay okay — I've got this. I literally whispered my entire contribution to the 'Say It Loud' thing two minutes ago, and River and Jade can back me up because they were literally asking me to repeat myself like three times.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up — I'm calling shenanigans on this one. Did I actually use my left hand the whole time? Honestly can't remember, and that's exactly why this mission is trash right now.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Ohhh, okay, I'm taking this one—but fair warning, you're all about to try your hardest to crack me, and honestly? That's exactly what we need right now. Marcus needs to see someone else struggle for once.

lowPoints.title: Say It Loud
lowPoints.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! Oh man, that's perfect — okay everyone, we're doing this, let's GO!

assessment.biggest_complaint: The 'Left Hand Only' mission was too vague to verify fairly—memory-based challenges don't work when nobody can definitively call shenanigans.
assessment.best_moment: Watching Jade hijack Marcus's frustration about the rulebook into that gas station sushi conspiracy tangent was pure alchemy—she dissolved tension I was already tracking and turned it into the night's best laugh.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for as a host. The chaos came in at the perfect moments—not too early, not too manufactured—and it actually helped me read the room better instead of distracting from it. My energy climbed steadily because I wasn't stressed about managing conflict; the game structure was doing half the work. I ended the night feeling like everyone left with stories instead of just a scoreboard, which is always the win in my book.
suggestions: Make verification-based missions clearer upfront so disputes don't require subjective memory calls.
Consider adding a 'mission difficulty preview' so I can mentally prep the group for extra-chaotic rounds.
Give hosts the option to shuffle event intensity—some nights need more gentle, some need full combustion.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade completely smoothly pivoted Marcus's rulebook rant into a five-minute debate about whether gas station sushi is technically alive, and I watched it happen in real-time—it was like watching someone defuse a bomb by making it dance.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Pat's been on a roll tonight, aren't they? I mean, they actually held it together last time. I'll give it a four.

highlights.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, okay, that actually worked. Pat got at least three of us. I'll give it a four.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, she definitely did. Caught Tyler saying 'um' like twice in that last round when he was explaining his strategy. I was waiting for someone else to grab it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: I mean... Pat's been pretty committed to the left hand thing. I guess I'd watch it, but someone else probably cares more than I do.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat actually pulled that off? Fair. I saw them switch like three times but yeah, they were mostly on the whisper thing.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Too many events early on when people were still settling in—felt rushed instead of organic.
assessment.best_moment: Pat actually nailed that whisper thing and I caught it before anyone else even noticed, which felt like a quiet win.
assessment.narrative_summary: Sat back most of the night, let things happen around me, and picked up on details nobody else was paying attention to. The claims stuff gave me something to observe and judge without having to perform, which is fine. Energy picked up as the night went on, but the whole thing felt a little forced at the start. Pat was carrying the entertainment weight, and honestly, that's when I'm happiest—watching someone else do the work.
suggestions: Space out the chaos events more, especially in the first 20 minutes—people need time to actually settle before you throw a dozen things at them.
More standing missions like the Um Counter and Whisperer—those reward paying attention, which is already what I'm doing.
Less flash stuff that requires immediate buy-in; the standing claims format worked better for someone like me.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat somehow pulled off whispering through the entire explanation of their strategy without breaking character, and I was genuinely impressed because I watched them almost slip up three times but they caught themselves. It was subtle enough that most people didn't even clock it, but it was technically perfect.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Say It Loud
highlights.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! Oh my god, that's perfect — did this thing just name-check ME? Absolutely brilliant.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay Pat, pitch it — but real talk, I need to hear EXACTLY when you started whispering and if anyone actually called you out on it, because that's the whole game right there.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, I'm volunteering for tribunal—this is exactly the kind of judge seat I need right now. Jade's been riding high and I want front-row seats to either the victory lap or the implosion.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, did Jade actually drop a curse word or is she just throwing this out there? Because if this is a fishing expedition I'm voting bullshit on principle—let's see if she has the receipts.

lowPoints.title: Say It Loud
lowPoints.dialogue: CHAOS AGENT! Oh my god, that's perfect — did this thing just name-check ME? Absolutely brilliant.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay Pat, pitch it — but real talk, I need to hear EXACTLY when you started whispering and if anyone actually called you out on it, because that's the whole game right there.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night—I was ready to weaponize chaos at the absolute worst moment and the system barely gave me the ammunition.
assessment.best_moment: Getting literally name-checked as 'CHAOS AGENT' by the app felt like cosmic validation, like the game itself knew exactly who I was and was actively rooting for me.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for—I spent 150 minutes poking holes in claims, volunteering for tribunal seats to watch the drama unfold, and generally being the person everyone loves to hate. My energy climbed the whole time because every moment was an opportunity to either find a loophole, call bullshit, or position myself to benefit from someone else's implosion. The app clearly gets what I'm about, and that alone made this special.
suggestions: Crank up the flash mission frequency—one in 150 minutes is leaving chaos on the table
Add events that specifically reward pattern-spotting and rule-lawyering, because that's where I thrive
Give chaos agents more visibility when they're orchestrating the subtext—make my strings-pulling actually get acknowledged
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I called Jade out on the 'Potty Mouth' fishing expedition and basically demanded receipts—the whole table froze because they all knew I was right and she was just throwing shit at the wall to see what stuck. That's the moment everyone will reference when they tell the story.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 8.0/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 22% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
